taxonomy: Pilorea graminifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Pilorea and is native to the Pacific Islands. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. Pilorea graminifolia is also known to be a member of the order Gentianales and the order's suborder Gentianineae. The specific classification of Pilorea graminifolia highlights its unique position among other flowering plant species.
